title: Bulk stub generation for missing locale keys
consumer_purpose: Developers need to auto-fill missing locale keys with [LANG] markers so the app is usable immediately without breaking parity tests
claim_atoms: script fills all 17 locales' missing keys
parity test passes after running script
content:
## Plan: L10N-003 — Bulk stub generation for missing locale keys

### Problem
L10N-002 added a parity test that enforces all 17 locales have all 971+ keys from en.ts. Any missing key causes CI failure. Adding a new locale or a new key requires manually adding every key to every locale — tedious and error-prone.

### Solution
Create a standalone Bun script at `packages/app/scripts/stub-locales.ts` that:
1. Reads `en.ts` and extracts all key names via regex
2. For each of the 17 locale files, extracts existing keys
3. Computes the diff (keys in en but not in locale)
4. Generates stub lines with `[LOCALE_CODE]` markers and `// TODO: translate` comments
5. Inserts them into the locale file, sorted alphabetically, preserving existing content

### File structure
- `packages/app/scripts/stub-locales.ts` — the script
- `packages/app/package.json` — add `"stub-locales": "bun run scripts/stub-locales.ts"` script entry

### Script design
- Uses Bun APIs (`Bun.file().text()`, `Bun.write()`)
- Exports no modules — standalone CLI script
- Takes no arguments; operates on all 17 locales
- Parse logic:
  - Extract keys from en.ts and each locale file using regex `/^\s+"([^"]+)":/`
  - Build `Set<string>` of existing locale keys
  - Filter enKeys not in locale set → missing keys
  - Sort missing keys alphabetically
- File rewrite logic:
  - Read locale file into lines array
  - Find the index of the closing `}` 
  - Identify existing key lines and their positions
  - Generate stub lines: `  "${key}": "[LOCALE_CODE]",  // TODO: translate`
  - Insert them before the closing `}`
  - Ensure proper trailing commas on all entries
- Locale code derived from filename (e.g., `da.ts` → `da`, `zht.ts` → `zht`)

### Guardrails
- Only reads en.ts for keys — does not modify it
- For each locale file, preserves ALL existing content including formatting, comments, blank lines
- Only adds missing keys — never modifies or removes existing translation values
- Does NOT touch parity.test.ts or any test/utility files
- Does NOT touch fallback.ts, plural.ts, format.ts

### Verification
- Run the script
- Run `bun test` in packages/app — parity tests must still pass
- Verify via `git diff` that only locale files were modified, and only additions were made

### Out of scope
- No actual translation work
- No changes to Postgres, IPC, or plugins
- No changes to the fallback mechanism or formatting logic
